tasks.description: Set up whale monitoring for a DeFi token with alert rules and response protocols
tasks.input: Set up a whale monitoring system for a DeFi lending protocol on Ethereum. The protocol has $50M TVL, 2,000 unique depositors, and a governance token with 100M circulating supply. The top 10 wallets hold 35% of the token supply. We want to detect: (1) whale accumulation/distribution patterns that could move the price, (2) large deposits to exchanges that signal selling pressure, (3) any security anomalies with the protocol's treasury multisig. Our team is 2 people and we use Telegram for alerts. Design the monitoring system.

scoring.5: Creates a tiered watchlist: Tier 1 for treasury multisig and deployer (real-time), Tier 2 for top 10 whale wallets and smart money (near real-time), Tier 3 for exchange hot wallets and top 50 holders (periodic); classifies wallets by entity type; addresses the 35% concentration risk; includes address clustering to catch multi-wallet whales
scoring.3: Has a watchlist but tiers are unclear or entity classification is incomplete; missing concentration risk analysis
scoring.1: Flat list of addresses without tiering or classification

scoring.5: Defines specific alert rules with relative thresholds (% of supply, not $ amounts): whale-to-exchange transfer >0.5% supply, multi-whale accumulation (3+ wallets buying same token in 4h), treasury multisig ANY non-whitelisted transaction, unusual token approval events; each rule has severity, cooldown, and enrichment; targets <10 alerts/day; includes internal transfer filtering to reduce false positives
scoring.3: Has alert rules but thresholds are absolute (dollar-based), or missing severity levels, or too many rules that would cause alert fatigue
scoring.1: Generic 'alert on large transfers' without specific rules

scoring.5: Specific security rules for the treasury multisig: alert on any signer change, threshold change, unexpected transaction, or interaction with unknown contracts; monitors token approval exposure; includes deployer wallet monitoring; addresses dependency monitoring (oracle, DEX integrations); has an emergency response protocol for critical alerts
scoring.3: Some security monitoring but misses key vectors (approvals, dependency monitoring) or lacks emergency protocol
scoring.1: Security mentioned but no specific monitoring rules

scoring.5: Designs for a 2-person team: recommends specific tools (Forta bots for security, Arkham/Nansen for labeling, custom Telegram bot for alerts); defines on-call rotation; separates real-time critical alerts (SMS/call) from batch digests (daily Telegram summary); suggests automation for common analysis tasks; realistic maintenance schedule
scoring.3: Acknowledges team size but system requires more effort than 2 people can sustain
